mechanism_narrative: A restrictive federal funds rate above 5% increases the discount rate for future corporate earnings and tightens financial conditions, leading to a contraction in equity multiples and negative forward returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the FEDFUNDS rate is above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strength in nonfarm payrolls growth reflects a robust economic backdrop that supports corporate revenue and earnings growth. This fundamental strength translates into positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where retail sales (RSAFS) are growing while housing starts (HOUST) are contracting signals a late-cycle environment. The interest-rate sensitive housing sector leads the broader economy, predicting a future slowdown that will negatively impact equities.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in RSAFS > 0% while 6-month change in HOUST < 0%
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production indicates improving supply-side conditions and corporate operating leverage. This positive momentum in output is a direct driver of earnings surprises and leads to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime, a low unemployment rate forces the central bank to maintain a hawkish policy stance. This combination of a tight labor market (UNRATE < 5%) and persistent inflation (rising CPIAUCSL) removes the 'Fed put' and predicts negative equity returns.
failure_condition: When the trigger conditions are met, the average 20-day forward return for the S&P 500 is positive over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate rising above a recent trough (e.g., > 0.3pp above its 12-month low) signals a turning point in the labor market. This leading indicator of recession, known as the Sahm Rule, predicts a broad economic downturn and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Following a trigger event, the S&P 500 fails to produce negative average 60-day returns over the subsequent 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP is the broadest measure of economic health. A positive quarter-over-quarter growth rate in GDPC1 indicates an expanding economy, which provides a fundamental tailwind for aggregate corporate earnings and supports positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: The sign of quarterly real GDP growth has a concordance of less than 55% with the sign of S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high inflation, as measured by the level of CPIAUCSL, erodes real returns and corporate profit margins. When the price level is elevated, it signals that monetary policy will remain tight, suppressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The information coefficient of the CPIAUCSL level predicting 20-day forward returns is non-negative over a 5-year rolling window.
